shared_patterns.pattern: longer, more complex sentences with multiple clauses
repeated mention of app crashing or freezing
complex sentence structures with multiple clauses
use of qualifiers like 'sometimes' and 'unexpectedly'
vocabulary related to troubleshooting and technical issues
short, direct questions or statements
use of lowercase at the beginning of sentences
concise descriptions of issues
requests for action or resolution
frequent use of urgency in language
informal greeting or opener (e.g., 'hi', 'do you have')
direct questions about specific issues (e.g., discounts, refunds, api limits)
concise and straightforward structure
use of lowercase for beginnings of sentences
mention of timeframes or expectations (e.g., '24h', '4 days')
short, abrupt sentences
direct complaints or requests
lack of emotional depth
use of informal language and abbreviations
repetitive structure in expressing frustration
concise technical issues
direct requests for assistance
use of abbreviations (e.g., mfa, api)
specificity in problem description
use of a casual opener (e.g., 'hi team, quick one')
repeated use of 'when i try to' structure
frequent use of conversational language and rhetorical questions
variations on similar themes of app issues and refunds
use of casual language and abbreviations (e.g., 'hey', 'q', 'lol')
longer, more complex sentence structures with multiple clauses
frequent use of 'i' statements indicating personal experience
questions posed in a conversational tone
use of conversational openers like 'so,' and 'but'
questions posed to the reader, creating a dialogue feel
repetitive structure in describing issues (e.g., 'the app crashes when...')
emotional language and personal anecdotes
